steps.title: Cook the rice
steps.text: Cook mixed rice according to packet instructions.

steps.title: Mind the rice timing
steps.text: Brown and black rice may need slightly different times — check the packet.

steps.title: Caramelise the onion
steps.text: Thinly slice onion and cook in olive oil over low heat for 10–12 minutes, stirring in 1 tsp honey halfway, until soft, golden and sweet.

steps.title: Make the lemon dressing
steps.text: Whisk lemon juice and zest with 3 tbsp olive oil, a pinch of salt and za'atar.

steps.title: Toast the pine nuts (optional)
steps.text: Toast the pine nuts in a dry pan over medium heat for 2–3 minutes until golden. Optional, but adds a lovely crunch — watch closely, they burn fast.
steps.tip: Skip for a nut-free version, or swap for toasted pistachios or flaked almonds.

steps.title: Combine & finish
steps.text: Mix cooled rice with caramelised onion, olives, semi-dried tomatoes and dressing.

steps.title: Toss well
steps.text: Toss well.

steps.title: Top with crumbled feta
steps.text: Top with crumbled feta, fresh mint, parsley and the toasted pine nuts (if using).

steps.title: Sauté the aromatics
steps.text: Warm a generous glug of olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the diced onion and carrots and cook for 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft and translucent.
steps.tip: Don't rush this step — softening the vegetables builds the flavour base.

steps.title: Add garlic & spices
steps.text: Add the minced garlic, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika and turmeric and cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.

steps.title: Add lentils, tomatoes & stock
steps.text: Rinse the lentils well, then add them to the pot with the chopped tomatoes and vegetable stock.

steps.title: Simmer
steps.text: Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer and cook for 20–25 minutes until the lentils are completely tender.
steps.tip: Red lentils dissolve for a creamy soup. Green lentils hold their shape for a chunkier version.

steps.title: Finish with greens & lemon
steps.text: Stir in the kale or spinach and cook for 2 minutes until wilted, then add a generous squeeze of lemon juice.

steps.title: Taste & serve
steps.text: Taste and adjust the seasoning — the lemon is essential, don't skip it.
Cook brown rice according to packet instructions. Fluff with a fork and set aside.
steps.title: Cook the rice

steps.tip: Cut into even cubes so everything roasts at the same time.
steps.text: Preheat oven to 200°C. Peel and cube sweet potato, toss with olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, salt and pepper. Roast 20–25 min until golden.
steps.title: Roast the sweet potato

steps.text: Thinly slice red onion and soak in red wine vinegar with a pinch of salt for 10 minutes. This takes the edge off and adds brightness.
steps.title: Quick pickle the onion

steps.text: Drain and rinse black beans. Add to a pan with corn and a pinch of cumin, warm through for 3 minutes.
steps.title: Warm the beans & corn

steps.text: Layer rice, sweet potato, beans and corn. Top with sliced avocado, pickled onion and fresh coriander. Squeeze lime over everything.
steps.title: Build the bowl

steps.text: Rinse quinoa well and cook in double the amount of water for 15 minutes. Drain and fluff.
steps.title: Cook the quinoa

steps.tip: Massaging kale makes it tender and much easier to eat.
steps.text: Remove tough stems, tear kale into pieces. Drizzle with olive oil and a pinch of salt and massage with your hands for 2 minutes until softened.
steps.title: Massage the kale
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can help massaging the kale! 🧒

steps.text: Warm honey in a small pan with a pinch of chilli flakes for 1 minute. Remove from heat.
steps.title: Make hot honey

steps.text: Thinly slice green apple and cucumber. Blanch edamame in salted water for 3 minutes, then drain.
steps.title: Prep the toppings

steps.text: Spread hummus as the base. Add quinoa and kale. Layer apple, cucumber, edamame on top. Crumble feta and drizzle with hot honey.
steps.title: Build the bowl

steps.text: Slice mushrooms and cook in olive oil over medium-high heat for 5–6 minutes until golden. Season with salt and pepper.
steps.title: Sauté the mushrooms

steps.text: Slice beetroot and apple thinly. Grate carrot. Halve cherry tomatoes.
steps.title: Prep the vegetables

steps.text: Roughly crush pistachios with the back of a knife or in a bag with a rolling pin.
steps.title: Crush the pistachios
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can smash the pistachios in a bag! 🧒

steps.tip: Cold water plus a touch of maple keeps the tahini from tasting bitter — add water slowly until it ribbons off the spoon.
steps.text: Whisk the tahini with the lemon juice, olive oil, grated garlic and a little maple syrup, then loosen with cold water a tablespoon at a time until creamy and pourable. Toss the greens with half, layer everything in the bowl, finish with sliced avocado and the pistachio crunch, then drizzle over the rest.
steps.title: Make the dressing & build

steps.text: Cube sweet potato, toss with olive oil and salt, roast at 200°C for 20 minutes until golden.
steps.title: Roast the sweet potato

steps.text: Halve and core the pear. Cook cut-side down in a pan with a little butter and 1 tbsp honey over medium heat for 3–4 minutes until golden.
steps.title: Caramelise the pear

steps.text: Add walnuts to a dry pan over medium heat for 3 minutes, tossing often. Watch carefully — they burn fast.
steps.title: Toast the walnuts

steps.text: Whisk remaining honey with grated ginger, a splash of apple cider vinegar and 2 tbsp olive oil.
steps.title: Make ginger honey dressing

steps.text: Layer arugula, sweet potato and pear. Crumble goat cheese on top. Scatter walnuts and cranberries. Drizzle dressing over everything.
steps.title: Assemble

steps.tip: Marinate overnight in the fridge for even deeper flavour.
steps.text: Mix olive oil, smoked paprika, minced garlic, chopped chilli, oregano, salt and lime juice. Coat chicken thighs and marinate for at least 10 minutes.
steps.title: Marinate the chicken

steps.text: Cook brown rice according to packet instructions.
steps.title: Cook the rice

steps.tip: Let it rest 3 minutes before slicing so the juices stay in.
steps.text: Cook chicken in a hot griddle pan or frying pan for 6–7 minutes per side until cooked through and nicely charred.
steps.title: Grill the chicken

steps.text: Add corn to the same pan for 2–3 minutes until slightly charred.
steps.title: Toast the corn

steps.text: Layer rice, sliced chicken and corn. Add halved cherry tomatoes and fresh coriander. Squeeze lime over the top.
steps.title: Assemble

steps.text: Cook brown rice according to packet instructions.
steps.title: Cook the rice

steps.text: Mix Dijon mustard, lemon juice, honey, minced garlic and a drizzle of olive oil into a smooth glaze.
steps.title: Make the glaze

steps.tip: Don't move the salmon while the skin is cooking — let it crisp up.
steps.text: Season salmon with salt and pepper. Cook in olive oil skin-side down for 4 minutes, flip, brush generously with glaze and cook 3 more minutes.
steps.title: Cook the salmon

steps.text: Cook green beans in boiling salted water for 3–4 minutes until just tender. Drain and season.
steps.title: Steam the green beans

steps.text: Layer rice and green beans. Place salmon on top, spoon over any remaining glaze and finish with fresh dill.
steps.title: Plate up

steps.tip: The drier the chickpeas, the crispier the falafel.
steps.text: Drain chickpeas well and pat dry. Blend with parsley, garlic, cumin, coriander, a pinch of salt and 2 tbsp flour until a rough paste forms. Don't over-blend — it should have some texture.
steps.title: Make the falafel mix

steps.text: Roll the mixture into small balls or patties. Refrigerate for 15 minutes — this helps them hold together.
steps.title: Shape & rest
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can shape the falafel balls! 🧒

steps.text: Heat olive oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Cook falafel for 3–4 minutes per side until deep golden and crispy.
steps.title: Fry the falafel

steps.text: Mix tahini with lemon juice, a clove of garlic and 2–3 tbsp water until smooth and pourable.
steps.title: Make tahini sauce

steps.text: Warm flatbreads. Top with sliced tomato, cucumber, falafel and a generous drizzle of tahini sauce.
steps.title: Assemble

steps.text: Press tofu for 15 minutes between kitchen paper. Cube and marinate in soy sauce, lemon juice and chilli flakes for 10 minutes.
steps.title: Press & marinate the tofu

steps.text: Boil baby potatoes in salted water for 15 minutes until just tender. Drain well.
steps.title: Boil the potatoes

steps.tip: The smashing is what makes them extra crispy!
steps.text: Place potatoes on a baking tray. Smash each one with the bottom of a glass. Drizzle with olive oil and roast at 220°C for 20 minutes until golden and crispy.
steps.title: Smash & roast
steps.kidsHelp: Kids love smashing the potatoes! 🧒

steps.text: Cook tofu in olive oil over high heat for 3–4 minutes per side until crispy and golden.
steps.title: Fry the tofu

steps.text: Wilt spinach in the same pan for 1 minute. Serve potatoes and tofu with spinach. Top with fresh herbs and a squeeze of lemon.
steps.title: Finish & serve

steps.tip: Make double — chimichurri keeps for 3 days and goes with everything.
steps.text: Finely chop parsley, garlic and chilli. Mix with olive oil, red wine vinegar, oregano and a pinch of salt. Let rest for at least 10 minutes.
steps.title: Make the chimichurri

steps.text: Cook brown rice according to packet instructions.
steps.title: Cook the rice

steps.tip: Let the steak rest for 5 minutes before slicing — this keeps it juicy.
steps.text: Season steak generously with salt and pepper. Cook in a very hot pan or griddle for 3 minutes per side for medium-rare. Adjust time to your preference.
steps.title: Grill the steak

steps.text: Slice steak against the grain. Layer rice and arugula, add steak slices and spoon chimichurri generously over the top.
steps.title: Slice & assemble

steps.text: Add saffron to warm water and leave for 5 minutes. Cook rice with this saffron water for a golden, fragrant result.
steps.title: Cook saffron rice

steps.text: Mix soy sauce, lime juice and a pinch of ginger. Set aside.
steps.title: Make the dressing

steps.tip: Use the freshest tuna you can find for this recipe.
steps.text: Coat tuna in sesame seeds. Sear in a very hot pan with olive oil for 1 minute per side — the centre should remain rare.
steps.title: Sear the tuna

steps.text: Slice tuna thinly. Arrange over rice with thin cucumber slices. Spoon dressing over and finish with coriander.
steps.title: Slice & serve

steps.text: Thinly slice red onion and cook in olive oil over low heat for 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally until soft and golden.
steps.title: Caramelise the onion

steps.text: Halve and scoop avocado into a bowl. Smash with a fork, add lemon juice, salt and chilli flakes.
steps.title: Smash the avocado
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can smash the avocado! 🧒

steps.tip: The fresher the egg, the better it holds together when poaching.
steps.text: Bring a deep pan of water to a gentle simmer with a splash of white vinegar. Create a gentle swirl, drop in the egg and cook for 3 minutes. Repeat for second egg.
steps.title: Poach the eggs

steps.text: Toast sourdough. Spread avocado generously, add caramelised onion and the poached egg. Top with dukkah, chives and a drizzle of olive oil.
steps.title: Toast & assemble

steps.tip: Keep the bowl in the freezer for 5 minutes before filling to stay cold longer.
steps.text: Break up frozen açaí and blend with oat milk and half the banana until thick and smooth. It should be spoonable, not drinkable — add less milk for a thicker result.
steps.title: Blend the açaí base

steps.text: Slice remaining banana and strawberries.
steps.title: Prepare the toppings
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can arrange the toppings however they like! 🧒

steps.text: Pour açaí base into chilled bowls. Arrange banana, berries, granola and coconut flakes on top. Drizzle with almond butter and a touch of honey if desired.
steps.title: Build & serve

steps.text: Combine oats, chia seeds, oat milk, maple syrup, lemon zest and a pinch of salt in a jar or bowl. Stir well.
steps.title: Mix the oats
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can do the mixing! 🧒

steps.tip: Make 5 jars on Sunday evening for the whole week.
steps.text: Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours or overnight. The oats will absorb the liquid and become thick and creamy.
steps.title: Refrigerate overnight

steps.text: In the morning, stir the oats and add a splash more milk if too thick. Top with blueberries, almond butter drizzle and coconut flakes.
steps.title: Top & serve

steps.text: Remove outer leaves, keeping the cauliflower whole. Rub all over with olive oil, garlic, cumin, salt and pepper.
steps.title: Prep the cauliflower

steps.tip: Cover with foil for the first 20 minutes then uncover to brown.
steps.text: Place on a baking tray and roast at 200°C for 30–35 minutes, until deeply golden and tender when pierced with a knife.
steps.title: Roast

steps.text: Mix tahini, lemon juice and 2–3 tbsp water until smooth and pourable. Season with salt.
steps.title: Make the tahini sauce

steps.text: Toast hazelnuts in a dry pan for 3 minutes. Roughly chop.
steps.title: Toast the hazelnuts

steps.text: Place roasted cauliflower on a platter. Drizzle tahini and harissa over the top. Scatter hazelnuts and coriander.
steps.title: Plate & finish

steps.text: Cook baby potatoes in salted water for 15–18 minutes until tender.
steps.title: Boil the potatoes

steps.text: Drain and place on a baking tray. Smash each one flat with a glass. Drizzle with olive oil, add crushed garlic, rosemary, salt and pepper.
steps.title: Smash & season
steps.kidsHelp: Kids love smashing the potatoes! 🧒

steps.tip: The key is a very hot oven and not overcrowding the tray.
steps.text: Roast at 220°C for 20–25 minutes until the edges are deeply golden and crispy.
steps.title: Roast until crispy

steps.text: Mix vegan mayo with a few drops of truffle oil (start small — it's strong). Taste and adjust.
steps.title: Make truffle mayo

steps.text: Scatter potatoes with flaky sea salt and fresh chives. Serve with truffle mayo on the side.
steps.title: Serve

steps.text: Peel and cube pumpkin. Toss with olive oil, cinnamon, salt and pepper. Roast at 200°C for 25–30 minutes until caramelised.
steps.title: Roast the pumpkin

steps.text: Rinse and cook quinoa in double the amount of water for 15 minutes.
steps.title: Cook the quinoa

steps.tip: They crisp up very quickly — watch carefully.
steps.text: Heat olive oil in a small pan over medium-high heat. Add sage leaves and fry for 30–60 seconds until crispy. Drain on paper towels.
steps.title: Crisp the sage

steps.text: Toast pumpkin seeds in a dry pan for 2–3 minutes until they start to pop.
steps.title: Toast the seeds

steps.text: Whisk tahini, maple syrup and lime juice with 2 tbsp water. Layer quinoa and pumpkin in bowls. Drizzle sauce and top with crispy sage and toasted seeds.
steps.title: Make maple tahini & assemble

steps.text: Mix lemon zest, lemon juice, minced garlic, chopped herbs, olive oil, salt and pepper. Coat chicken and leave for 10 minutes.
steps.title: Marinate the chicken

steps.text: Place cherry tomatoes on a tray with olive oil and salt. Roast at 200°C for 15 minutes until burst and juicy.
steps.title: Roast the tomatoes

steps.tip: Rest the chicken before slicing to keep it juicy.
steps.text: Pan-fry chicken over medium heat for 6–7 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Rest 3 minutes before slicing.
steps.title: Cook the chicken

steps.text: Cook green beans in boiling salted water for 3–4 minutes. Drain and toss with a drizzle of olive oil.
steps.title: Steam the green beans

steps.text: Slice chicken and serve alongside green beans and roasted tomatoes.
steps.title: Plate up

steps.text: Halve tomatoes and place on a tray with whole garlic cloves, olive oil, chilli flakes, salt and pepper. Roast at 200°C for 20 minutes until burst and slightly caramelised.
steps.title: Roast the tomatoes

steps.tip: Salty pasta water is the secret to a good sauce.
steps.text: Cook pasta in well-salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve a cup of pasta water before draining.
steps.title: Cook the pasta

steps.text: Squeeze roasted garlic out of the skins and mash into the tomatoes. Toss with drained pasta, adding pasta water a splash at a time to loosen.
steps.title: Combine

steps.text: Tear fresh basil over the top and grate parmesan generously. Drizzle with extra olive oil.
steps.title: Finish & serve
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can tear the basil leaves! 🧒

steps.text: Dice onion and pepper. Cook in olive oil over medium heat for 5–6 minutes until soft. Add garlic and cook 1 more minute.
steps.title: Sauté the vegetables

steps.text: Stir in cumin, smoked paprika and chilli flakes. Cook 1 minute, then add chopped tomatoes. Simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
steps.title: Add the spices & tomatoes

steps.tip: Keep the lid on — this steams the top of the eggs gently.
steps.text: Make 4 wells in the sauce. Crack one egg into each well. Cover and cook over low heat for 5–7 minutes until whites are set but yolks still runny.
steps.title: Poach the eggs

steps.text: Crumble feta over the top, scatter fresh parsley and drizzle with olive oil. Serve directly from the pan with bread.
steps.title: Finish & serve

steps.text: Dice onion and cook in olive oil over medium heat for 5 minutes. Add garlic and thyme, cook 2 more minutes.
steps.title: Sauté aromatics

steps.text: Drain and rinse white beans. Add to the pan with roasted peppers, smoked paprika and vegetable stock.
steps.title: Add beans & peppers

steps.text: Bring to a boil and simmer for 15 minutes.
steps.title: Simmer

steps.tip: Blend in batches if your blender is small.
steps.text: Blend the soup until smooth. Add lemon juice, adjust seasoning. Serve with a drizzle of olive oil.
steps.title: Blend & finish

steps.text: Dice onion and cook in olive oil for 5 minutes. Add garlic, grated ginger, cumin and turmeric. Cook 2 minutes.
steps.title: Sauté the aromatics

steps.text: Peel and chop carrots into chunks. Add to the pan with rinsed red lentils and vegetable stock.
steps.title: Add carrots & lentils

steps.text: Bring to a boil, then simmer for 20 minutes until carrots and lentils are completely soft.
steps.title: Simmer

steps.text: Blend smooth. Stir in coconut milk and warm through. Season and serve with fresh coriander.
steps.title: Blend & finish

steps.tip: Wear gloves when peeling!
steps.text: Wrap whole beetroot in foil and roast at 200°C for 35–40 minutes until tender. Cool, peel and cut into wedges.
steps.title: Roast the beetroot

steps.text: Toss walnuts with honey in a pan over medium heat for 2 minutes until glazed. Spread on parchment to cool.
steps.title: Candy the walnuts

steps.text: Whisk olive oil, balsamic, lemon juice, salt and pepper.
steps.title: Make the dressing

steps.text: Arrange arugula and beetroot. Segment orange over the top. Crumble goat cheese and scatter candied walnuts. Drizzle with dressing.
steps.title: Plate & serve

steps.text: Preheat oven to 180°C. Slice onion, mushrooms and garlic. Cook in olive oil in an oven-safe frying pan for 5–6 minutes.
steps.title: Preheat & sauté

steps.text: Add spinach to the pan and stir until wilted, about 1 minute.
steps.title: Wilt the spinach

steps.text: Whisk eggs with salt, pepper and thyme. Pour over the vegetables in the pan.
steps.title: Beat the eggs
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can crack and whisk the eggs! 🧒

steps.tip: The frittata keeps for 3 days in the fridge — great for meal prep.
steps.text: Crumble feta on top. Bake in the oven for 12–15 minutes until just set in the centre.
steps.title: Bake

steps.text: Cook asparagus and peas in boiling salted water for 2–3 minutes until just tender and bright green. Drain and run under cold water.
steps.title: Blanch the vegetables

steps.text: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice and a splash of balsamic. Season well.
steps.title: Make the dressing

steps.text: Roughly crush pistachios.
steps.title: Crush the pistachios

steps.text: Arrange vegetables on a plate. Tear burrata open and place in the centre. Drizzle dressing over everything. Scatter pistachios and a pinch of Maldon salt.
steps.title: Plate & serve

steps.text: Pierce sweet potatoes all over with a fork. Rub with olive oil and bake at 200°C for 40–45 minutes until completely soft.
steps.title: Bake the sweet potatoes

steps.tip: The drier the chickpeas, the crispier they get.
steps.text: Drain and dry chickpeas very well. Toss with olive oil, cumin and salt. Roast on a tray at 200°C for 25–30 minutes until crispy.
steps.title: Crisp the chickpeas

steps.text: Mix tahini with lemon juice and 2–3 tbsp water until smooth and pourable.
steps.title: Make tahini sauce

steps.text: Split sweet potatoes open. Fill with crispy chickpeas, drizzle tahini, scatter pomegranate seeds, parsley and chilli flakes.
steps.title: Load & serve

steps.text: Season chicken with oregano, salt and pepper. Pan-fry in olive oil for 6–7 minutes per side. Rest and slice.
steps.title: Cook the chicken

steps.tip: Eat halloumi straight away — it gets rubbery as it cools.
steps.text: Slice halloumi and cook in a dry pan over high heat for 1–2 minutes per side until golden.
steps.title: Grill the halloumi

steps.text: Halve cherry tomatoes, slice cucumber and chop herbs. Make a simple dressing with lemon juice, olive oil, salt and pepper.
steps.title: Prepare the salad

steps.text: Toss greens with dressing. Top with chicken, halloumi, olives and fresh herbs.
steps.title: Assemble

steps.text: Cut all vegetables into similar-sized pieces so they roast evenly.
steps.title: Prep the vegetables
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can help washing and sorting the vegetables! 🧒

steps.tip: Don't overcrowd — space means roasting not steaming.
steps.text: Toss everything with olive oil, salt, pepper and thyme. Spread on a large baking tray — give everything space. Roast at 200°C for 25–30 minutes.
steps.title: Season & roast

steps.text: Whisk wholegrain mustard, lemon juice and olive oil together.
steps.title: Make the dressing

steps.text: Arrange vegetables on a plate and drizzle with mustard dressing.
steps.title: Serve

steps.tip: Dissolve the matcha first to avoid lumps.
steps.text: Whisk matcha into a small amount of warm oat milk until dissolved. Add oats, remaining oat milk, yogurt, honey and a pinch of salt. Stir well.
steps.title: Mix the base

steps.text: Cover and chill for at least 6 hours.
steps.title: Refrigerate overnight

steps.text: In the morning stir and top with banana slices, granola and hemp seeds. Dust with a pinch of matcha.
steps.title: Top & serve

steps.text: Mix Greek yogurt with grated garlic and a pinch of salt. Spread over plates and bring to room temperature.
steps.title: Make the garlic yogurt

steps.text: Bring a deep pan of water to a gentle simmer with a splash of vinegar. Swirl and carefully drop in the eggs. Cook 3 minutes for runny yolks.
steps.title: Poach the eggs

steps.tip: Use good chilli flakes — they make all the difference here.
steps.text: Melt butter in a small pan. Add chilli flakes and cook 30 seconds until sizzling and fragrant.
steps.title: Make chilli butter

steps.text: Place poached eggs on the yogurt. Drizzle hot chilli butter immediately over the top. Scatter fresh dill. Serve with toasted sourdough.
steps.title: Assemble

steps.tip: Wear gloves — beetroot stains!
steps.text: Slice cooked beetroot thinly or into wedges. Dress with a splash of balsamic, olive oil and lemon juice.
steps.title: Slice the beetroot

steps.text: Toast hazelnuts in a dry pan for 3 minutes. Roughly crush.
steps.title: Toast the hazelnuts

steps.text: Spread labneh over a plate or board. Arrange beetroot, berries and tomatoes on top. Scatter hazelnuts and fresh dill. Drizzle with olive oil.
steps.title: Plate

steps.tip: Salt aubergine first and leave 10 minutes to draw out moisture for better texture.
steps.text: Slice aubergine into 1cm rounds. Brush with olive oil and salt. Grill or griddle for 3–4 minutes per side until charred and tender.
steps.title: Grill the aubergine

steps.text: Drain and dry chickpeas. Toss with harissa and olive oil. Roast at 200°C for 20 minutes.
steps.title: Roast the chickpeas

steps.text: Mix tahini with lemon juice and water until pourable.
steps.title: Make tahini sauce

steps.text: Arrange aubergine on a plate. Spoon over harissa chickpeas. Drizzle tahini, scatter pomegranate and coriander.
steps.title: Assemble

steps.text: Cook pasta in well-salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve a cup of pasta water before draining.
steps.title: Cook the pasta

steps.text: Toast pine nuts in a dry pan for 2–3 minutes until golden. Watch carefully — they burn fast.
steps.title: Toast the pine nuts

steps.text: Cook garlic in olive oil for 1 minute. Add spinach and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes.
steps.title: Wilt the spinach

steps.text: Add drained pasta to the pan. Stir in ricotta, lemon juice and zest, and a splash of pasta water to loosen. Toss well.
steps.title: Combine

steps.text: Divide into bowls. Grate parmesan generously on top and scatter pine nuts.
steps.title: Serve
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can grate the parmesan! 🧒

steps.text: Whisk miso, honey, soy sauce and a splash of sesame oil together.
steps.title: Make the miso glaze

steps.text: Cook jasmine rice according to packet instructions.
steps.title: Cook the rice

steps.text: Brush salmon with miso glaze. Cook in a pan over medium heat for 4 minutes skin-side down, then flip, brush again and cook 3 more minutes.
steps.title: Glaze & cook the salmon

steps.text: Cook bok choy in the same pan with a splash of sesame oil and soy sauce for 2 minutes until just wilted.
steps.title: Wilt the bok choy

steps.text: Layer rice, bok choy and salmon. Scatter sesame seeds and squeeze lime over the top.
steps.title: Plate & finish

steps.text: Chop courgette, pepper and tomatoes. Toss with drained chickpeas, olive oil, smoked paprika and salt. Roast at 200°C for 20–25 minutes.
steps.title: Roast the vegetables

steps.text: Toast flatbreads in a dry pan or directly over a gas flame for 30 seconds per side.
steps.title: Warm the flatbread

steps.text: Spread hummus generously in bowls. Pile the warm roasted veg on top. Drizzle with olive oil, scatter parsley and a pinch of paprika.
steps.title: Assemble
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can spread the hummus! 🧒

steps.tip: The riper the banana, the sweeter the bread — use very spotty black bananas for best results.
steps.text: Preheat oven to 175°C. Mash bananas in a large bowl until smooth.
steps.title: Preheat & mash
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can mash the bananas! 🧒

steps.text: Whisk eggs, melted coconut oil, oat milk, 5–6 tbsp maple syrup and vanilla into the mashed banana.
steps.title: Mix wet ingredients

steps.text: Add flour, baking soda, cinnamon and salt. Stir gently until just combined — don't overmix. Fold in most of the walnuts.
steps.title: Fold in dry ingredients
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can fold in the walnuts! 🧒

steps.tip: Cover with foil after 30 minutes if the top browns too quickly.
steps.text: Pour into a lined loaf tin. Scatter remaining walnuts on top. Bake for 50–55 minutes until a skewer comes out clean.
steps.title: Bake

steps.tip: Don't overcook prawns — they turn rubbery quickly.
steps.text: Season prawns with salt and pepper. Cook in olive oil over high heat for 1–2 minutes per side until pink and just cooked.
steps.title: Cook the prawns

steps.text: Dice mango, slice avocado and cucumber. Finely chop chilli.
steps.title: Prepare the fruit & veg

steps.text: Whisk lime juice, olive oil, a pinch of salt and a drop of fish sauce (optional) together.
steps.title: Make the dressing

steps.text: Combine greens, mango, avocado, cucumber and chilli. Add prawns and drizzle dressing. Top with coriander.
steps.title: Toss & serve

steps.text: Cube sweet potato, toss with olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin and salt. Roast at 200°C for 20 minutes.
steps.title: Roast the sweet potato

steps.text: Thinly slice red onion and soak in red wine vinegar and a pinch of salt for 10 minutes.
steps.title: Quick pickle the onion

steps.text: Mash avocado with lime juice and salt until smooth.
steps.title: Make avocado crema
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can smash the avocado! 🧒

steps.text: Drain and rinse black beans. Warm in a pan with cumin, salt and a splash of water.
steps.title: Warm the beans

steps.text: Warm tortillas. Spread avocado crema, add beans and sweet potato, top with cabbage, pickled onion and coriander. Squeeze lime over everything.
steps.title: Assemble the tacos
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can build their own tacos! 🧒

steps.tip: Use fresh carrots — pre-grated ones are too thick and dry.
steps.text: Finely grate carrots using the small holes of a box grater for seamless batter texture.
steps.title: Grate the carrots
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can help with the grater with supervision! 🧒

steps.text: Combine oats, flour, 1 tsp cinnamon, ½ tsp ginger, 1 tsp baking powder, ½ tsp nutmeg and a pinch of salt.
steps.title: Mix dry ingredients
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can do the stirring! 🧒

steps.tip: A few lumps are fine — overmixing makes them dense.
steps.text: Whisk eggs, milk, applesauce and maple syrup together. Fold into dry ingredients. Don't overmix. Fold in grated carrot.
steps.title: Mix wet & combine

steps.tip: If batter feels too thick, add 2–3 tbsp extra milk — oats absorb a lot of liquid.
steps.text: Heat a non-stick pan with a little butter over medium heat. Pour 8cm rounds and cook 2–3 minutes until bubbles form, then flip and cook 1–2 minutes more.
steps.title: Cook the pancakes

steps.tip: Stores in the fridge for 3 days or freeze for 3 months.
steps.text: Stack pancakes and top with Greek yogurt, sliced strawberries, blueberries and a drizzle of maple syrup.
steps.title: Serve with yogurt & berries
steps.kidsHelp: Let kids add the fruit and yogurt! 🧒

steps.tip: Roasting concentrates all the tomato flavour.
steps.text: Halve tomatoes and place cut-side up on a baking tray with whole garlic cloves, olive oil, salt and a pinch of sugar. Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes.
steps.title: Roast the tomatoes

steps.text: Sauté diced onion in olive oil for 5 minutes. Add rinsed lentils and stock. Simmer 20 minutes.
steps.title: Cook the lentils

steps.text: Add roasted tomatoes and garlic to the lentils. Blend smooth. Season and warm through.
steps.title: Blend together

steps.text: Ladle into bowls. Drizzle olive oil and scatter fresh basil.
steps.title: Serve

steps.text: Halve tomatoes, slice courgette. Arrange on a baking tray with sliced garlic and olives. Drizzle with olive oil and season.
steps.title: Prep the vegetables

steps.text: Roast at 200°C for 15 minutes.
steps.title: Roast the veg first

steps.text: Nestle cod fillets on top of the vegetables. Season with salt, pepper and lemon slices. Return to the oven for 10–12 minutes until cod flakes easily.
steps.title: Add the cod

steps.text: Scatter fresh thyme and serve directly from the tray.
steps.title: Finish & serve

steps.text: Cook broccoli and edamame in boiling salted water for 3 minutes. Drain and rinse with cold water to keep them bright green.
steps.title: Blanch the broccoli & edamame

steps.text: Remove tough stems. Tear kale and massage with olive oil and a pinch of salt for 2 minutes until softened.
steps.title: Massage the kale
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can help massaging! 🧒

steps.text: Whisk lemon juice, grated ginger, apple cider vinegar and olive oil together.
steps.title: Make the dressing

steps.text: Combine kale, sliced cucumber, broccoli and edamame. Top with sliced avocado. Drizzle dressing and scatter hemp seeds.
steps.title: Assemble

steps.tip: Thin, even strips mean crispy fries.
steps.text: Cut sweet potato into thin strips. Toss with olive oil, smoked paprika and salt. Roast at 220°C for 25–30 minutes, flipping halfway.
steps.title: Make sweet potato fries

steps.text: Slice chicken into strips. Toss with olive oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt and pepper.
steps.title: Season the chicken

steps.text: Cook in a hot pan for 3–4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
steps.title: Cook the chicken

steps.text: Mix Greek yogurt with grated garlic, lemon juice and chopped parsley.
steps.title: Make the dipping sauce
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can mix the dipping sauce! 🧒

steps.text: Plate chicken and sweet potato fries. Serve with yogurt dip and fresh parsley.
steps.title: Serve

steps.text: Cook brown rice in double the water for 20 minutes. Rest 5 minutes with the lid on.
steps.title: Cook the rice

steps.tip: Give the florets space on the tray — they roast not steam.
steps.text: Preheat oven to 200°C. Toss broccoli florets with olive oil, salt and pepper. Roast 15–18 minutes until edges are golden.
steps.title: Roast the broccoli

steps.text: Blend tahini, lemon juice, garlic and parsley with 3 tbsp water until creamy. Blanch edamame 3 minutes in salted water, then rinse cold.
steps.title: Blend the dressing

steps.text: Layer rice, broccoli, edamame. Add avocado slices and cucumber ribbons. Drizzle dressing and finish with sunflower seeds.
steps.title: Build the bowl

steps.tip: 7 minutes gives a perfectly jammy yolk.
steps.text: Bring water to a boil. Lower eggs in and cook exactly 7 minutes. Transfer to ice water, then peel.
steps.title: Soft boil the eggs

steps.text: Cook green beans in boiling salted water for 3 minutes. Drain and rinse cold.
steps.title: Blanch the green beans

steps.text: Season tuna with salt and pepper. Sear in a very hot pan for 1 minute per side. If using tinned, simply drain.
steps.title: Sear the tuna (if fresh)

steps.text: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, salt and pepper together.
steps.title: Make the dressing

steps.text: Arrange greens, tomatoes, beans and olives. Place tuna and halved eggs on top. Drizzle with dressing.
steps.title: Plate

steps.text: Mix turkey mince with breadcrumbs, one egg, half the garlic (minced), oregano, salt and pepper. Roll into small balls.
steps.title: Make the meatballs
steps.kidsHelp: Kids love rolling meatballs! 🧒

steps.text: Fry meatballs in olive oil over medium-high heat for 5–6 minutes, turning until browned all over.
steps.title: Brown the meatballs

steps.text: In the same pan, sauté diced onion and remaining garlic for 3 minutes. Add chopped tomatoes. Simmer 10 minutes.
steps.title: Make the sauce

steps.text: Return meatballs to the sauce. Simmer together for 10 more minutes.
steps.title: Simmer together

steps.text: Cook spaghetti until al dente. Serve with meatballs, sauce, fresh basil and grated parmesan.
steps.title: Serve with pasta
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can grate the cheese! 🧒

steps.tip: The less milk, the thicker the bowl — add a tablespoon at a time.
steps.text: Blend frozen berries and frozen banana with just enough oat milk to get it moving. Keep it thick — it should be almost solid. Add milk gradually.
steps.title: Blend the base

steps.text: Slice kiwi and strawberries.
steps.title: Prepare the toppings
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can arrange the toppings! 🧒

steps.text: Pour thick base into bowls. Top with kiwi, strawberries, granola, coconut flakes and chia seeds.
steps.title: Build & serve

steps.text: Halve and core pears. Place cut-side up in a baking dish with a knob of butter and 1 tbsp honey. Roast at 200°C for 18–20 minutes until golden.
steps.title: Roast the pears

steps.text: Blend or finely chop pistachios with olive oil, remaining honey and a pinch of salt to a rough paste.
steps.title: Make pistachio dressing

steps.text: Spoon stracciatella onto plates. Top with warm roasted pears. Drizzle pistachio oil, scatter mint and finish with flaky sea salt.
steps.title: Plate & serve

steps.text: Preheat oven to 200°C. Slice peppers in half lengthways and remove seeds. Brush with olive oil and roast for 15 minutes.
steps.title: Prep the peppers

steps.text: Sauté diced onion and garlic in olive oil for 5 minutes. Add drained lentils, cumin, paprika, salt and halved cherry tomatoes. Cook 5 minutes.
steps.title: Make the filling

steps.text: Spoon lentil filling into pepper halves. Crumble feta on top. Bake 20 minutes until peppers are tender and feta is golden.
steps.title: Stuff & bake
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can help spooning the filling! 🧒

steps.text: Scatter fresh parsley over the top and serve warm.
steps.title: Serve

steps.text: Break cauliflower into florets. Toss with olive oil and salt. Roast at 210°C for 22–25 minutes until golden.
steps.title: Roast the cauliflower

steps.text: Toast almonds in a dry pan for 3 minutes. Roughly chop.
steps.title: Toast the almonds

steps.text: Blend lemon juice, garlic, Dijon mustard, 3 tbsp olive oil and a pinch of salt until smooth.
steps.title: Make the Caesar dressing

steps.text: Tear romaine into large pieces. Add warm cauliflower. Toss with dressing. Shave parmesan generously and scatter almonds.
steps.title: Assemble

steps.text: Cook rice and season with a splash of rice vinegar and a pinch of salt.
steps.title: Cook the rice

steps.text: Season salmon and cook in sesame oil, skin-side down, for 4 minutes. Flip and cook 3 more minutes.
steps.title: Cook the salmon

steps.text: Mix soy sauce, lime juice, a drop of sesame oil and grated ginger.
steps.title: Make the dressing

steps.text: Add rice to bowls. Top with sliced salmon, avocado, cucumber and edamame. Spoon dressing and scatter sesame seeds.
steps.title: Build the bowl

steps.text: Cook diced onion and garlic in olive oil for 4 minutes until soft.
steps.title: Sauté aromatics

steps.tip: Don't overcook — the vibrant green colour fades quickly.
steps.text: Add peas and stock. Bring to a boil and cook for 3–4 minutes — no more, to keep the colour bright.
steps.title: Add peas & stock

steps.text: Add fresh mint and blend smooth. Season with lemon juice, salt and pepper.
steps.title: Blend with mint

steps.tip: Roasting is what gives this soup its incredible depth of flavour — don't skip it.
steps.text: Break cauliflower into florets. Toss with olive oil, salt and pepper on a large tray — give them space. Roast at 200°C for 25–30 minutes until deeply golden on the edges.
steps.title: Roast the cauliflower

steps.text: Cook diced onion in olive oil for 5 minutes. Add garlic and thyme, cook 2 more minutes.
steps.title: Sauté onion & garlic

steps.text: Add roasted cauliflower and stock to the onion pan. Bring to a simmer for 5 minutes then blend until completely smooth. Add cream and a pinch of nutmeg.
steps.title: Blend

steps.text: Toast pine nuts in a dry pan 2–3 minutes. Season soup with lemon juice and salt. Serve with toasted pine nuts and a drizzle of good olive oil.
steps.title: Finish & serve

steps.tip: Spread them out — they need space to roast not steam.
steps.text: Cube sweet potato and drain chickpeas. Toss both with olive oil, smoked paprika and salt on a baking tray. Roast at 200°C for 22–25 minutes.
steps.title: Roast sweet potato & chickpeas

steps.text: Whisk tahini, lemon juice and 2–3 tbsp water until smooth.
steps.title: Make tahini dressing

steps.text: Layer arugula, warm sweet potato and chickpeas. Crumble feta, scatter pomegranate and drizzle with tahini dressing.
steps.title: Assemble
Melt the chocolate
steps.text: Gently melt the 85% dark chocolate (water bath or short microwave bursts), then let it cool slightly.

steps.title: Blend smooth
steps.text: Blend the avocado, peanut butter, maple syrup, sugar, cocoa, almond milk, vanilla and a pinch of salt with the melted chocolate until completely smooth and glossy.
steps.tip: The 85% chocolate + peanut butter give it a deep, fudgy richness.

steps.title: Caramelise the pistachios
steps.text: In a small dry pan, toast the pistachios with the honey over medium heat for 1–2 minutes until glossy and caramelised, then tip onto baking paper to cool and harden.

steps.title: Chill, top & serve
steps.text: Spoon the mousse into 2 glasses and chill for at least 2 hours. Just before serving, top with the raspberries and the caramelised pistachios.
Halve tomatoes, drizzle with olive oil and salt. Roast at 200°C for 12 minutes.
steps.title: Roast the tomatoes

steps.text: Cook spinach in a pan with olive oil for 1–2 minutes until just wilted. Season.
steps.title: Wilt the spinach

steps.text: Bring a deep pan of water to a gentle simmer with a splash of white vinegar. Poach eggs for 3 minutes for runny yolks.
steps.title: Poach the eggs

steps.text: Toast sourdough. Layer with spinach, roasted tomatoes and olives. Top with poached eggs. Crumble feta and finish with fresh basil and olive oil.
steps.title: Assemble

steps.text: Cook brown rice according to packet instructions.
steps.title: Cook the rice

steps.tip: Thin slices cook faster and more evenly.
steps.text: Slice chicken into thin strips. Chop broccoli and pepper. Mince garlic and grate ginger. Have everything ready before you start — stir-fries move fast.
steps.title: Prep everything first

steps.text: Heat sesame oil in a wok or large pan over very high heat. Add chicken and cook 3–4 minutes until golden. Remove and set aside.
steps.title: Cook the chicken

steps.text: In the same pan, cook garlic and ginger for 30 seconds. Add broccoli, pepper and snap peas. Stir-fry 3–4 minutes on high heat.
steps.title: Stir-fry the veg

steps.text: Return chicken to the pan. Add soy sauce and toss everything together. Serve over brown rice.
steps.title: Bring it together

steps.tip: Black rice takes longer than white — start it first.
steps.text: Rinse black rice and cook in double the amount of salted water for 30–35 minutes until tender. Drain well.
steps.title: Cook the black rice

steps.text: Slice peppers in half lengthways, remove seeds. Brush with olive oil, season and roast at 200°C for 15–20 minutes until just softened.
steps.title: Roast the peppers

steps.tip: The sweet raisins and salty feta together are the magic of this dish.
steps.text: Mix warm black rice with crumbled feta, roughly crushed pistachios, raisins, sliced spring onion, cinnamon, cumin and lemon juice. Toss with olive oil. Taste and season.
steps.title: Make the filling

steps.text: Fill pepper halves generously with the rice mixture. Return to the oven for 5 minutes. Scatter fresh mint and parsley over the top before serving.
steps.title: Stuff & finish
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can help spooning in the filling! 🧒

steps.text: Toast sourdough until golden and crispy.
steps.title: Toast the bread

steps.text: Season ricotta with a tiny pinch of salt. Spread generously over toast.
steps.title: Spread the ricotta
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can spread the ricotta! 🧒

steps.tip: Eat immediately — toast softens quickly with the ricotta.
steps.text: Slice figs and arrange on top. Crush pistachios and scatter over. Drizzle honey generously and finish with fresh thyme leaves.
steps.title: Top & drizzle

steps.tip: Cooling the quinoa before dressing prevents it from going mushy.
steps.text: Rinse quinoa in a fine mesh sieve. Cook in double the amount of water with a pinch of salt for 15 minutes. Drain any excess water and fluff with a fork. Spread on a tray to cool — this keeps the grains separate.
steps.title: Cook the quinoa

steps.text: Dice red pepper and cucumber. Thinly slice red onion. Drain and rinse chickpeas.
steps.title: Prep the vegetables

steps.text: Whisk lemon juice, lemon zest, minced garlic, olive oil, salt and pepper together. Taste — it should be bright and quite sharp, it will mellow when tossed.
steps.title: Make the dressing

steps.tip: Add dressing just before serving if prepping ahead.
steps.text: In a large bowl combine cooled quinoa, chickpeas, vegetables and most of the parsley. Pour over dressing and toss well. Top with remaining parsley. Keeps well for 3 days in the fridge.
steps.title: Combine & serve

steps.tip: Low and slow is the only way. High heat burns instead of caramelises.
steps.text: This is the heart of mujadara — don't rush it. Thinly slice onions and cook in olive oil over medium-low heat for 30–35 minutes, stirring every few minutes, until deeply golden and jammy. Season with salt halfway through.
steps.title: Caramelise the onions

steps.text: Rinse lentils and cook in salted water for 15 minutes until just tender. Drain and set aside.
steps.title: Cook the lentils

steps.text: In a pot, toast cumin, cinnamon and allspice in olive oil for 1 minute. Add rinsed rice and cooked lentils. Add water to cover by 2cm. Bring to a boil then simmer covered for 15 minutes.
steps.title: Cook the spiced rice

steps.text: Fluff the lentil rice and layer in bowls. Top generously with caramelised onions. Squeeze lemon over and scatter fresh parsley. Serve with plain yogurt on the side.
steps.title: Combine & serve

steps.text: Rinse and cook quinoa in double the water for 15 minutes. Spread out to cool.
steps.title: Cook the quinoa

steps.text: Finely shred red cabbage. Grate or julienne carrots. Blanch edamame 3 minutes and drain. Thinly slice spring onions.
steps.title: Prep the vegetables

steps.tip: The dressing should be creamy but pourable. Keep adding water gradually.
steps.text: Whisk peanut butter, lime juice, soy sauce, sesame oil, a little maple syrup, minced garlic and grated ginger. Add warm water, 1 tbsp at a time, until pourable and smooth.
steps.title: Make the peanut dressing

steps.text: Combine quinoa, all vegetables and half the coriander. Pour over dressing and toss well. Top with remaining coriander and crushed roasted peanuts.
steps.title: Toss & serve

steps.tip: Grate raw sweet potato — no need to cook it first.
steps.text: Preheat oven to 200°C. Grease a 12-hole muffin tin or line with paper cups. Grate sweet potato using the coarse side of a box grater.
steps.title: Preheat & prep
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can help filling the muffin tin! 🧒

steps.text: In a large bowl whisk together buckwheat flour, oat flour, 2 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp ground ginger and ½ tsp sea salt.
steps.title: Mix dry ingredients

steps.tip: The dates sweeten naturally — no sugar needed.
steps.text: In a blender, combine eggs, spinach, pitted dates, yogurt, olive oil and lemon zest. Blend until completely smooth and bright green.
steps.title: Blend wet ingredients
steps.kidsHelp: Kids love watching it turn green! 🧒

steps.tip: A few lumps are fine. Overmixing makes them dense.
steps.text: Pour the green wet mixture into the dry ingredients. Stir until just combined — don't overmix. Fold in the grated sweet potato.
steps.title: Combine & fold in sweet potato

steps.tip: Keeps 3 days in an airtight container or freeze for up to 3 months.
steps.text: Divide batter evenly into the muffin tin. Top each with a few pumpkin seeds. Bake for 22–25 minutes until a skewer comes out clean and the tops are golden.
steps.title: Bake

steps.tip: Farro should be al dente — slightly chewy is perfect.
steps.text: Cook farro in plenty of salted boiling water for 25–30 minutes until tender but still chewy. Drain well.
steps.title: Cook the farro

steps.text: Cube courgette and aubergine. Toss with cherry tomatoes, olive oil, oregano, salt and pepper on a baking tray. Roast at 200°C for 20–25 minutes.
steps.title: Roast the vegetables

steps.text: Toast pine nuts or walnuts in a dry pan for 2–3 minutes until golden.
steps.title: Toast the nuts

steps.text: Whisk lemon juice, a splash of balsamic and olive oil. Toss warm farro with roasted vegetables and dressing. Crumble feta and scatter basil and nuts on top.
steps.title: Make the dressing & combine

steps.text: Rinse buckwheat groats well under cold water — this removes any bitterness.
steps.title: Rinse the buckwheat

steps.tip: Add extra milk if it thickens too much — consistency should be like creamy oat porridge.
steps.text: Add rinsed buckwheat to a pan with oat milk, cinnamon, vanilla and a pinch of salt. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 12–15 minutes, stirring regularly, until creamy and the groats are tender.
steps.title: Cook the porridge

steps.tip: Swap the fruit with whatever is in season — stewed pear in winter, mango in summer.
steps.text: Spoon into bowls. Top with fresh raspberries, sliced banana, mixed seeds and a drizzle of maple syrup.
steps.title: Top & serve
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can arrange their own toppings! 🧒

steps.text: Cook pasta in well-salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve a cup of pasta water before draining.
steps.title: Cook the pasta

steps.tip: Adjust harissa to taste — add more for heat, less for kids.
steps.text: Cook minced garlic in olive oil for 1 minute. Add harissa paste and cook 1 more minute. Add chopped tomatoes and simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
steps.title: Make the harissa tomato sauce

steps.tip: Room temperature yogurt won't split when it hits the hot pasta.
steps.text: Mix Greek yogurt with lemon juice, lemon zest and a pinch of salt. Keep at room temperature.
steps.title: Make the lemon yogurt

steps.text: Toss drained pasta into the tomato sauce, adding pasta water to loosen. Serve in bowls with a generous spoonful of lemon yogurt swirled on top. Scatter fresh parsley and grate cheese over.
steps.title: Combine & serve

steps.tip: A splash more oat milk at the end loosens it to your liking.
steps.text: Simmer the oats with oat milk, a pinch of salt and ½ tsp cinnamon for 5–7 minutes, stirring, until creamy.
steps.title: Cook the porridge

steps.text: Cube the apples and cook in a little butter or coconut oil with cinnamon and 1 tbsp maple syrup for 4–5 minutes until soft and golden. Toast the pecans in a dry pan for 2–3 minutes, then roughly chop.
steps.title: Sauté the cinnamon apples
steps.kidsHelp: Kids can stir the cinnamon apples! 🧒

steps.text: Spoon the porridge into bowls, top with the cinnamon apples, pecans and raisins, then drizzle with maple syrup and almond butter.
steps.title: Build & serve
